backup_api_paths: GET / (ListBuckets -> ListAllMyBucketsResult / ListBucketResult XML over the S3 REST API on :9878)
GET /{bucket} (ListObjectsV2, path-style; ?list-type=2&prefix=&max-keys=&delimiter= enumerates keys without downloading)
http://host:9874/ (Ozone Manager web UI - volume/bucket namespace)
http://host:9888/ (Recon management console - cluster + bucket metadata; CVE-2021-41532 unauth pre-1.2.0)
ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML data from NAMES/MANIFEST only. (1) List volumes/buckets via GET / on :9878 or the OM web UI on :9874 and read the ListAllMyBucketsResult/ListBucketResult <Name> values; AI-store tells are bucket or volume names like models, model-registry, checkpoints, datasets, training-data, mlflow, mlflow-artifacts, artifacts, feature-store, embeddings, ray, kubeflow, spark-warehouse, iceberg/hive warehouse paths, dvc, lakefs. (2) Enumerate keys with GET /{bucket}?list-type=2&prefix=&delimiter=/ and read the <Key> values only: extensions .ckpt .safetensors .pt .pth .onnx .gguf .bin model.safetensors config.json tokenizer.json, MLmodel / mlflow run-id prefixes, parquet/feature partitions, or an OCI layout (oci-layout, index.json, blobs/sha256/) whose manifest mediaType is application/vnd.ml.model.* or a model artifact. Reading the bucket/key NAMES and an index.json/manifest is in-scope inventory; downloading the referenced object blobs is exfil and out of scope.

dorks.strict: "<Error>" "Error creating s3 auth info" "AWS V4 signing" port:9878 -product:MinIO
dorks.version: http.html:"ozone.s3g" OR http.title:"S3 Gateway" port:19878 "Apache Ozone"

fingerprint_markers: S3 REST API on tcp/9878 (HTTP) / 9879 (HTTPS), path-style addressing default (http://host:9878/bucketname)
XML root element ListBucketResult / ListAllMyBucketsResult with namespace http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/ (S3Consts.S3_XML_NAMESPACE)

Ozone-specific unsigned-request rejection: HTTP InvalidRequest with message 'Error creating s3 auth info. The request may not be signed using AWS V4 signing algorithm, or might be invalid' (returned to a bare browser GET, distinct from MinIO/AWS wording)
S3G webadmin bucket-browser UI on tcp/19878 (ozone.s3g.webadmin.http-address) - separate port from the S3 API, unlike MinIO which serves console + API together
Co-located Hadoop/Ozone web UIs on the same host: Ozone Manager :9874 (ozone.om.http-address), SCM :9876, Recon :9888 - Hadoop-stack ports absent from any MinIO/Ceph RGW deployment
Embedded Jetty server from hadoop-common (no 'Server: MinIO' / 'Server: AmazonS3' header); JAX-RS Jersey 2 + Weld stack
Recon/OM JMX and /conf servlets on the web-UI ports return Hadoop-style com.codahale/Hadoop MBeans
fp_guards: Require an Ozone-specific signal, not a bare S3 200: match ListBucketResult AND (the 'AWS V4 signing' InvalidRequest message OR a co-located Ozone OM/SCM/Recon port). The S3 2006-03-01 XML namespace alone matches AWS, MinIO, Ceph RGW, SeaweedFS, Garage - do NOT label on it.
Exclude MinIO: drop hosts with Server: MinIO, x-minio-* headers, /minio/health/live, or a console on :9001. Ozone has none of these.
Exclude Ceph RADOS Gateway: drop Server: Ceph / x-amz-request-id of RGW form, and the :7480 default. Exclude SeaweedFS (:8333) and Garage (distinct error wording).
The webadmin UI lives on 19878, NOT 9878 - a port:9878 + http.title match for a bucket browser is the API port and likely a reverse-proxy artifact; verify the 19878 webadmin separately.

Anonymous 200 on GET / is NOT expected on a default-secure cluster; an open ListBuckets is the CVE-2020-17517 / misconfig finding, not the fingerprint. Distinguish 'fingerprinted Ozone S3G' from 'anon-listable Ozone S3G'.

LBot / catch-all deception fleets can echo ListBucketResult; require the vendor-unique InvalidRequest auth-info string or a real co-located OM/Recon port, and guard against a catch-all that 200s every path.

cves.desc: S3 Gateway missing authorization: in a secure Ozone cluster S3 buckets and keys were reachable by anonymous/unsigned HTTP requests (curl), exposing data to unauthenticated clients. Fixed in 1.1.0. Primary anon-listing vector for this layer.
Recon HTTP endpoints (:9888) exposed OM/SCM/Datanode metadata to any unauthenticated user before 1.2.0; cluster + bucket namespace metadata leak. Fixed 1.2.0.
Improper authentication (missing mutual TLS) on an internal SCM endpoint allowed downloading SCM-internal metadata without auth. Affects 1.2.0-1.3.0, fixed 1.4.0.

Improper authentication of an S3 Gateway HTTP endpoint in 1.4.0 let any authenticated Kerberos user revoke/regenerate another user's S3 secrets when ozone.s3g.secret.http.enabled=true (default false). Fixed 1.4.1 (endpoint disabled).
S3 credentials could be obtained by other users due to improper handling before 1.2.0 (S3 secret exposure).
Privileged SCM admin commands executable by any authenticated user (not just admins) before 1.2.0.
Internal server-to-server endpoints allowed an attacker to download raw data from Datanode and Ozone Manager before 1.2.0 (Critical).
ACL bypass / improper authorization in Ozone before 1.2.0.
Block token access weakness allowing unauthorized block reads before 1.2.0.

pivot_paths: GET / on :9878 -> ListAllMyBucketsResult: read volume/bucket NAMES only to scope AI stores (models/datasets/mlflow/checkpoints), no key reads
GET /{bucket}?list-type=2&prefix=&delimiter=/&max-keys=N -> read <Key> NAMES and <Size> to map artifact layout (page through ?continuation-token), never GetObject
Browse the :19878 webadmin bucket-browser UI read-only to enumerate buckets without crafting SigV4 requests
OM web UI :9874 -> volume/bucket inventory + quota/usage metadata (namespace-level, no object reads)
Recon console :9888 -> cluster topology, datanode and container counts, bucket metadata (CVE-2021-41532 surface if pre-1.2.0; read-only)
SCM web UI :9876 and /jmx, /conf servlets -> version + config fingerprint for CVE scoping (version-band only)
GET /{bucket}?acl -> read bucket ACL/owner displayName for operator attribution (list-only, no policy change)
restraint_note: Names-only. Inventory volume/bucket/key NAMES via ListBuckets and ListObjectsV2, and read an index.json/MLmodel manifest to classify, but never issue GetObject to download a checkpoint, dataset, or model blob - that is exfil. Do not PutObject/CreateBucket/DeleteObject, do not modify bucket ACLs or policies, do not call S3-secret generate/revoke endpoints. An open anonymous GET / is logged as 'surface open, listing exercised on names only'; data-class is asserted from manifests/names, never from object contents. Treat Recon/OM/SCM web UIs as read-only metadata reads.
